title: `UNION ALL` doesn't work as expected
description: Using a statement with `UNION ALL` in some cases doesn't produce any results
poc:
SELECT
	caseid as caseid,
	caseid as "delivery.caseid"
FROM
	"cases"
UNION ALL
SELECT
	caseid as caseid,
	cast(null as varchar(255)) as "delivery.caseid"
FROM
	"cases"
UNION ALL
SELECT
	caseid as caseid,
	cast(null as varchar(255)) as "delivery.caseid"
FROM
	"cases";
key_bug_features: `UNION ALL` fails to return expected results
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE "test_cases" (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, caseid VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, status BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE);
INSERT INTO "test_cases" (caseid, status) VALUES ('DL1-1', TRUE), ('DL1-2', FALSE), ('DL1-3', TRUE);
CREATE TABLE "delivery" (delivery_id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, caseid VARCHAR(255), delivery_date DATE);
SELECT caseid, status FROM "test_cases" WHERE status = TRUE UNION ALL SELECT caseid, CAST(NULL AS BOOLEAN) FROM "test_cases" WHERE status = FALSE;
